jokes.text: You can't go through with it, Boxy. For she is your sister.
jokes.punchline: The interruption reveals an incestuous relationship
jokes.explanation: Classic soap opera parody with good timing and economy

jokes.text: And my sister. And my wife! And my air conditioner!
jokes.punchline: The relationships keep escalating to include an appliance
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation joke that builds absurdity effectively

jokes.text: What in the name of my ass is going on here?
jokes.punchline: Unexpected replacement of 'God' with 'my ass'
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific profanity twist on common expression

jokes.text: That is an amazing invention. This isn't the invention, you boob.
jokes.setup: Amy admires what she thinks is the invention
jokes.punchline: Professor immediately deflates her with insult
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Professor's impatience

jokes.text: Let me just activate it using my footnological marvel, the toelonger. It's glorious! That's not the invention, either. Stop guessing.
jokes.punchline: Continues the pattern of people guessing wrong inventions
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the guessing game with absurd invention name

jokes.text: Hyello, Mr. Professorvich. Very extremely good catch for you today. Luckily was big railroad accident.
jokes.setup: A cheerful greeting from what seems like a vendor
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's selling human body parts from accidents
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor with perfect cheerful delivery contrasting grim content

jokes.text: Wow. Is that a flatlock seam? Why, thank you for noticing, Amy. It looks elegant and minimizes blood loss.
jokes.punchline: Professor's practical response about blood loss
jokes.explanation: Perfect juxtaposition of fashion terminology with medical practicality

jokes.text: Um, Professor? Most people have a head. Sure. The in-crowd.
jokes.setup: Amy points out the obvious missing head
jokes.punchline: Professor treats having a head as trendy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion treating basic human anatomy as a fashion trend

jokes.text: The head is the part with the teeth and the sneeze hole, right?
jokes.setup: Professor needs to identify what a head is
jokes.punchline: Calls nose a 'sneeze hole'
jokes.explanation: Good childish terminology for nose, fits Professor's absent-minded character

jokes.text: Can it hit a no-look jump shot? -Uh, sure. -Then why does it need me?
jokes.setup: Nixon asks about the body's basketball abilities
jokes.punchline: Logic that superior athletic ability makes him unnecessary
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific vanity joke with sound logic

jokes.text: Good one, Mommy!
jokes.setup: A child head responds to Nixon's joke
jokes.punchline: Inappropriately calls Nixon 'Mommy'
jokes.explanation: Mild confusion joke about disembodied child head

jokes.text: Ugh. Okay, but remember: Their eyes are up here.
jokes.setup: Standard 'eyes are up here' line about not staring at bodies
jokes.punchline: Ironic since they're looking at disembodied heads
jokes.explanation: Clever reversal of common phrase given the unusual situation

jokes.text: Um, do any of you lovely lady... People...
jokes.setup: Professor starts to address the heads
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly corrects himself unsure of proper term
jokes.explanation: Mild awkwardness joke about gender assumptions with heads

jokes.text: I miss doing runway shows and intimidating short people.
jokes.setup: Model expressing what she misses about having a body
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning intimidation as part of her job
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - treats intimidation as casual job perk rather than side effect

jokes.text: The left side's pretty tall. Trust me, it's attractive. It's got thick, luxurious hair all over the place, and several legs that just won't quit. And one that will.
jokes.setup: Professor describes the body's attractive features
jokes.punchline: One leg will quit, implying it's defective
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of typical sales pitch with honest flaw admission

jokes.text: Of course, dear, it's not the body, it's how you wear it.
jokes.punchline: Plays on 'it's not what you wear, it's how you wear it'
jokes.explanation: Clever adaptation of fashion saying to literal body-wearing situation

jokes.text: That's Cara Delevingne. She invented eyebrows.
jokes.punchline: Claims she invented a basic human feature
jokes.explanation: Absurd celebrity fact that plays on Delevingne's famous eyebrows

jokes.text: I'm sorry, Cara. But due to your various and unusual proportions, no clothing will ever fit you.
jokes.punchline: The visual of the bizarre Frankenstein body makes normal clothes impossible
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the clothing solution, works with visual comedy

jokes.text: You didn't need that hind leg anyway.
jokes.punchline: Professor casually dismisses the loss
jokes.explanation: Good casual dismissal of body horror, fits Professor's indifference

jokes.text: Thanks, but it's a little Soviet brutalist for me.
jokes.punchline: Compares fashion to architecture style
jokes.explanation: Clever fashion criticism using architectural terminology

jokes.text: I never expected to love something so much that was excreted from a moth.
jokes.setup: Cara expresses love for the dress
jokes.punchline: Reminds us of the gross biological origin
jokes.explanation: Good juxtaposition of fashion love with biological reality

jokes.text: Wernstrom!
jokes.punchline: Professor's predictable angry outburst
jokes.explanation: Classic callback to established character relationship

jokes.text: into lovable companions for the elderly.
jokes.punchline: Converting weapons of mass destruction into cute elderly companions
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor concept - turning apocalyptic weapons into grandparent helpers

jokes.text: Aww! Come along, Explodey.
jokes.punchline: The companion is named 'Explodey' revealing its dangerous origin
jokes.explanation: Perfect juxtaposition of cute pet name with ominous origin

jokes.text: Who made that dress? It's fabulous. Did you not hear what I said? I brought a human back to life!
jokes.punchline: Everyone only cares about the fashion
jokes.explanation: Good commentary on society's priorities, fashion over science

jokes.text: But that gown brings sexy back to science.
jokes.punchline: Plays on Justin Timberlake song title
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture reference but feels somewhat forced

jokes.text: In the spirit of recycling, we're reusing last year's envelope. And the winner was Ogden Wernstrom.
jokes.punchline: Announces last year's winner instead of this year's
jokes.explanation: Good logical consequence of the recycling gag

jokes.text: I've got a midlife crisis ponytail for a Hubert Farnsworth. It's pronounced Hugh-bear. It is?
jokes.setup: Professor corrects pronunciation of his name
jokes.punchline: Even he seems unsure if that's right
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Professor's fashion world pretensions

jokes.text: Gorgeous, like a desiccated Zac Posen.
jokes.punchline: Compares him to a dried-up designer
jokes.explanation: Decent fashion world insult disguised as compliment

jokes.text: I'm on the cover of Vague. My mother would be so confused.
jokes.punchline: Magazine is 'Vague' instead of 'Vogue', mother confusion
jokes.explanation: Good pun on fashion magazine name with character insight

jokes.text: Thank you. It's holding my neck on.
jokes.punchline: Reveals its literal life-sustaining function
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of horrific reality disguised as fashion talk

jokes.text: Being boneless, clothing never fits me. It's like trying to put pants on a waterbed.
jokes.punchline: Vivid simile about dressing a waterbed
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual metaphor that perfectly captures the challenge

jokes.text: I once got the pants off a waterbed, but that's a very long, very dirty story.
jokes.punchline: Implies sexual encounter with furniture
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender response, turning innocent metaphor sexual

jokes.text: Money's no object. I have contractile vacuoles full of it.
jokes.setup: Standard rich person line about money
jokes.punchline: Uses paramecium anatomy terminology
jokes.explanation: Brilliant integration of cellular biology into wealth bragging

jokes.text: Gol'durned cytoplasm. Yeah! That's it! Make sweet asexual love to the scanner.
jokes.punchline: Encourages 'asexual love' appropriate for paramecium
jokes.explanation: Good biology-appropriate version of typical photography direction

jokes.text: Make sweet asexual love to the scanner. No, don't shake your money maker. Give me less of that. Don't work it, baby.
jokes.punchline: Contradictory photography directions ending with telling her NOT to be sexy
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of photography clichés - reversing typical sexy pose directions

jokes.text: What, this little thing? It's from the House of Professor.
jokes.punchline: Professor treated as legitimate fashion house
jokes.explanation: Good fashion world parody with Professor as designer brand

jokes.text: Hugh-bear Farntsvirt
jokes.punchline: Increasingly mangled pronunciation
jokes.explanation: Mild escalation of name pronunciation running gag

jokes.text: Sorry, we're full. The anteater can come in, though.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg denied entry to fashion event
jokes.punchline: Random animal allowed in instead
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist fashion world superficiality

jokes.text: You're dressed like some sort of underpaid lobster doctor from outer space. But I can't afford nicer clothings.
jokes.punchline: Perfect description of exactly what Zoidberg is
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-joke that perfectly describes Zoidberg from outsider perspective

jokes.text: You can't come in our dumpster what looking like that. Not during Fashion Week.
jokes.punchline: Even homeless people in dumpster reject him
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing how universally Zoidberg is rejected

jokes.text: You can't come in our dumpster what looking like that. Not during Fashion Week.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg trying to throw himself away
jokes.punchline: Even the garbage has fashion standards during Fashion Week
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of fashion superficiality - even the trash is elitist during Fashion Week

jokes.text: Your fabulous clothing just makes the other 99% of us sad and jealous.
jokes.punchline: Accurate social commentary about fashion inequality
jokes.explanation: Good social commentary through character - Zoidberg articulating real class issues

jokes.text: What? No, I'm just going underwater where the fashion standards are lower.
jokes.punchline: Just seeking environment with lower standards
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that subverts dark expectations with character logic

jokes.text: Excuse me, the fashion standards aren't lower. Why, look at the flatlock seam on my clam shells.
jokes.punchline: References same seam type from earlier
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier fashion terminology joke

jokes.text: I've never told anyone this, but it's been my lifelong dream to be a famous scientist.
jokes.punchline: She already works with famous scientists daily
jokes.explanation: Good irony about Amy not recognizing her current situation

jokes.text: I've never told anyone this, but it's been my lifelong dream to be a famous scientist.
jokes.setup: Amy talking to Professor about dreams
jokes.punchline: Reveals dream to be what she already is - she's literally a scientist
jokes.explanation: Good irony - Amy not recognizing she's already achieved her supposed dream

jokes.text: Fly, fly, my pretties!
jokes.punchline: Wizard of Oz reference with flying monkeys
jokes.explanation: Standard pop culture reference applied to moth deployment

jokes.text: Good Mor-night. I will destroy you and your entire species if you continue to combine those words.
jokes.punchline: Morbo threatens genocide over word combination
jokes.explanation: Perfect Morbo response, extreme violence over minor linguistic annoyance

jokes.text: Good Morning Tonight. Or as I like to say, Good Mor-night.
jokes.punchline: Awkward word combination that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Mild TV host humor - the forced word combination is appropriately awkward

jokes.text: I will destroy you and your entire species if you continue to combine those words.
jokes.punchline: Morbo's violent overreaction to bad wordplay
jokes.explanation: Perfect Morbo response - his species-level threats over minor annoyances is classic character

jokes.text: This just in. I look fabulous.
jokes.punchline: Morbo reports on his own appearance
jokes.explanation: Perfect subversion of news format with alien vanity

jokes.text: These slacks really flatter Morbo's pulsating buttocks.
jokes.punchline: Grotesque alien anatomy description
jokes.explanation: Excellent contrast between fashion language and alien physiology

jokes.text: Oh, no need to wash anything. The outfits fall apart after one day's use. But they'd be out of fashion by then anyway.
jokes.punchline: Built-in obsolescence justified by fashion cycles
jokes.explanation: Sharp commentary on fast fashion and planned obsolescence

jokes.text: It's fast fashion at its fastiest.
jokes.punchline: Made-up superlative 'fastiest'
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay but fits Professor's pseudo-scientific language

jokes.text: Who knows? Who cares?
jokes.setup: Question about where disposed clothes go
jokes.punchline: Complete indifference to environmental impact
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of environmental negligence with efficient delivery

jokes.text: Anything can fit. The fash-can is a multi-dimensional wormhole through space and time and probably some other things.
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of incredibly complex physics
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of simple trash can into cosmic technology

jokes.text: The fash-can is a multi-dimensional wormhole through space and time and probably some other things.
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of incredibly complex physics for garbage disposal
jokes.explanation: Good casual sci-fi absurdity - treating complex physics as simple waste disposal

jokes.text: Relax. I get a new pet every day to go with my outfits. That's slightly worse.
jokes.punchline: Reveals daily pet disposal, Leela's horrified response
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from bad to worse with perfect reaction

jokes.text: I get a new pet every day to go with my outfits.
jokes.setup: Professor's casual attitude toward pet disposal
jokes.punchline: Treating living beings as fashion accessories
jokes.explanation: Dark character moment - Professor's callous treatment of life as fashion coordinate

jokes.text: If any civilization was here, it's been smothered by the Professor's clothes.
jokes.setup: Seeing planet covered in discarded clothes
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of civilizational extinction by fashion waste
jokes.explanation: Good environmental horror - treating extinction as side effect of fashion consumption

jokes.text: Deploy her? I barely know her. Just kidding. I'm a regular customer.
jokes.punchline: Sexual pun followed by admission of patronage
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender inappropriate sexual humor with perfect delivery

jokes.text: Actually, it's not the moths that eat clothes. It's the caterpillars. People don't know that because they're tiny and hard to see.
jokes.punchline: Leela delivers educational fact at inappropriate time
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leela's need to correct misconceptions even in danger

jokes.text: No. I am a professional fashion model. We do not run. We strut.
jokes.punchline: Professional standards prevent running, only strutting
jokes.explanation: Perfect character commitment to professional standards over survival

jokes.text: It won't stand up to even the lowest setting.
jokes.setup: Cara offering to iron wrinkled pants
jokes.punchline: Cheap material can't survive any heat at all
jokes.explanation: Good practical humor - the reality of cheap fast fashion quality

jokes.text: What is one life against a completely decent pair of pants?
jokes.punchline: Treating pants as worth more than human life
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist priorities and heroic sacrifice parody

jokes.text: Well, if everyone is already dead, I don't see the problem.
jokes.punchline: Complete moral indifference to mass extinction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor logic and moral blindness

jokes.text: But that's our planet, right? And we're not buried under miles of laundry.
jokes.setup: Revelation that destroyed planet is Earth
jokes.punchline: Fry's confusion about timeline paradox
jokes.explanation: Good Fry confusion about time travel consequences

jokes.text: Damn you all to TJ Maxx!
jokes.setup: Planet of the Apes style curse
jokes.punchline: Fashion-appropriate hell is discount retailer
jokes.explanation: Brilliant fashion-world appropriate curse with perfect Planet of Apes reference
